strengths: Clear identification of three distinct real-world objects with accompanying descriptions
Vivid and concrete descriptive language that aids visualization of artefacts
Logical structure and signposting (e.g., headers) that enhance clarity of the exhibition
weaknesses: Links between objects and the IA prompt remain surface-level and sometimes drift into moral commentary rather than TOK analysis
Insufficient integration of evidence and sparse use of explicit TOK terminology (ways of knowing, knowledge questions)
Contexts for some objects are generic rather than specific, and citations are not woven into the critical discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment situates the work firmly in the 3â4 band: the student names three objects and offers some context, but explanations of their relevance to the prompt are unfocused and supported by general assertions rather than rigorous evidence. Strengths include vivid description and clear organization, yet the analysis lacks explicit TOK concepts, deeper critical reflection and integrated citation. Given the official total of 4 and the overall performance matching the top of the 3â4 descriptor, a score of 4 accurately reflects the studentâs achievement in this single gradiable criterion.

annotations.comment: The vivid description of the book cover helps the reader visualize the artefact, which supports the overall exhibitionâs communicative effectiveness.
The student clearly identifies a concrete real-world object â a Bollywood biopic of Murlikant Petkar â providing a focused anchor for the exhibition.
The concluding paragraph summarizes each object superficially but does not synthesize insights across them nor draw an overarching TOK conclusion.
While a works cited list is provided, the exhibition would benefit from more consistent in-text citation integration to connect evidence directly to your analysis.
The analysis rarely employs explicit TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, areas of knowledge); integrating these concepts will deepen the sophistication of your argument.
The movie poster is a generic promotional image rather than a specific, personal artefact; consider using a still or original poster from a personal viewing to strengthen authenticity.
The studentâs header distinctly marks the transition to the second object, aiding the structural clarity of the exhibition.
The book cover is a common promotional image; consider selecting a more unique artefact (e.g., personal notes or an interview transcript) to illustrate how direct exposure shapes knowledge.
The context remains generic (a forthcoming film) without details of when, where or in what form this artefact was encountered as a unique experience; specify the particular screening or source.
The detailed image description demonstrates strong observational skills, which could support deeper analysis if tied back to the TOK prompt.
The citation is listed parenthetically but is not woven into the analysis; integrate source details into your argument to show how external information shapes your knowledge claims.
This narrative recounts Petkarâs life events descriptively but stops short of analysing how firsthand experience specifically transforms knowledge, as required by the prompt.
The opening statement is abrupt and disconnected from the object context; clarify how this initial comment relates to the specific artefact and your personal engagement with it.
Object 2 is labelled clearly, but lacks bibliographic context (e.g., edition, publication date, specific copy); include these to ground your analysis in a precise real-world artefact.
Embedding a hyperlink without authorâdate context weakens academic rigor; use consistent in-text citation style to attribute ideas and data properly.
The recount of George Floydâs death is factual but remains descriptive; you must explicitly analyse how personal narratives contribute to knowledge construction under the TOK framework.
The cited Fickman study is used as evidence, but its methodology and reliability are not evaluated; critically assessing source credibility is essential in TOK analysis.
The analysis omits key TOK terminology (ways of knowing, knowledge questions); incorporating these concepts would strengthen the conceptual rigour of your argument.
The discussion on racism shifts into moral commentary and general social critique, rather than maintaining focus on how racism as a personal experience specifically affects knowledge acquisition.
Object 3 is clearly identified with a precise description of the studentâs own photograph, making the artefact personal and immediately relevant to the prompt.
The photographâs provenance (date, laboratory conditions, personal role) is omitted; such specifics would reinforce how firsthand experimentation shapes knowledge.
The hypothesis description is procedural; it needs deeper reflection on how the experience of conducting the reaction informs understanding of scientific knowledge.
This section asserts that personal experience verifies knowledge but lacks exploration of knowledge justification or the limits and implications of using lived experience as evidence.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
